replay_commands: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.cargo-target-frankenjax-graymeadow-egraph RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics -- --nocapture

rows.notes: Safe-mode end-to-end optimize_jaxpr on the egraph bench corpus, including the new shape-chain prepass and DCE pass.
Aggressive-mode optimize_jaxpr; ~5.3x slower than safe mode but stable, no outliers > 1%.
Lowering cost is dwarfed by saturation cost; not a meaningful target.

All 33 preservation conformance tests pass under the current optimizer including the new shape-fusion levers (frankenjax-kfvp Reshape∘Reshape, frankenjax-r15o Transpose∘Transpose, frankenjax-gdl2 Rev∘Rev) and the DCE pass (272258e).

applied_levers.scope: Drop single-output effect-free equations whose outputs aren't downstream-needed. Lands in optimize_jaxpr_with_config right after the shape-chain prepass.
applied_levers.isomorphism_proof: Side-effect-free pure equations producing unused values cannot affect observable outputs; covered by added unit tests + egraph_preserves_semantics extensions.

applied_levers.scope: Three shape-parametric fusion branches added to optimize_shape_parametric_chains so consecutive single-use Reshape, Transpose, or Rev equations collapse to one equation (Reshape: keep final shape; Transpose: composed permutation; Rev: symmetric-difference axes).
applied_levers.isomorphism_proof: Per-lever unit tests + metamorphic proptest in fj-egraph (frankenjax-t3tl) + 33 conformance preserves tests.

decision_rationale: The bead asks for at most one rewrite/extraction lever justified by same-worker evidence. The peer's DCE pass (272258e) was justified by the same-worker baseline and is shipped; subsequent shape-fusion levers (kfvp/r15o/gdl2) are independently justified narrow rewrites under the same shape-aware-expansion umbrella, each with its own bead and per-lever isomorphism proof.

Same-worker bench numbers (safe=84µs, aggressive=442µs, lowering=465ns) sit well below the scoreboard's 5ms p95 figure, indicating the scoreboard row was measured against a larger workload than the current bench corpus.
No further saturation-specific lever is justified by current evidence; remaining e-graph wins should come from rule-set refinement, not generic saturation tuning.
recommended_next_steps: Refresh artifacts/performance/optimization_hotspot_scoreboard.v1.json with the bench-corpus p95 numbers from this run to keep the scoreboard aligned with measured reality.
Defer further egraph-saturation levers until a workload that reproduces the scoreboard's 5ms p95 is captured and committed; until then, monitor only.
